WARNING: These test points are for A3113 (820-03286) ONLY. Component designators are board-specific.
description: Decision trees for 20V DCPS scenarios. Primary diagnostic voltage.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws almost nothing. Port controller dead, charger IC not accepting input, or PPBUS_AON open.

steps.action: Check port controller (UF400) for signs of life
steps.measure: Probe PP3V3_UPCO_LDO or PP1V5_UPCO_LDO_CORE (internal LDO of UF400)

steps.if_missing: Charger IC not converting. Check CHGR_AUX_OK (page 31), SMC_I2C1 bus (page 57).

scenarios.meaning: Some current but below normal boot (1.5-2.5A). Shorted rail clamping a regulator or early boot stall.

steps.if_all_present: All main rails present. Fault in SoC domain or peripheral. Go to step 5.
steps.if_any_missing: Identify missing rail -> follow specific fault path.

steps.action: PP3V8_AON missing - investigate U5700 / short on 3V8
steps.if_shorted: PP3V8_AON shorted. Thermal cam. Check VDDMAIN branch, WLBT branch.

steps.measure: Check UC300 (LN84243-1) output and enable gating (UC637/UC630, page 52)
steps.note: A3113 uses LN84243-1 for UC300. P5VS2_PWR_EN gated by PMU_CRASH logic.

steps.action: MPMU LDO output missing - thermal cam
steps.measure: Thermal camera on the shorted/missing LDO rail area

steps.action: All rails present - check SoC and I2C
steps.measure: Check SMC_I2C1 bus (page 57), SoC reset signals
steps.note: I2C bus stuck can hold board in low-current state.

scenarios.meaning: Boot-level current but not completing. SoC likely powered but stalling during firmware/display/storage.

steps.action: Watch current pattern - stable or cycling?

steps.measure: Check NAND power: PP1V25_AWAKE_NAND_VCC (SPMU BUCK 6), PP3V8_AWAKE_NAND_VDD (SPMU BUCK 12 SW6/7)
steps.if_missing: NAND power fault. Check SPMU BUCK 6/12 outputs.
steps.if_present: NAND powered but SSD may be dead. Try DFU restore.

steps.measure: Check PPBUS_AONSW_LCD (UP950 output, page 84), San Diego display PMU (page 85)

steps.if_any_missing: MPMU buck feeding that domain failed.
steps.if_all_present: SoC powered -> firmware/SSD issue. DFU restore.

scenarios.meaning: NORMAL operating range for M3 MacBook Air at 20V. If customer reports fault at this current, issue is peripheral not power.

steps.if_display_working: Board boots. Fault not power-related. Check peripherals.

steps.measure: Check display chain: UP950 -> LP800 (BEN) -> JP600 connector -> San Diego PMU

sub_checks.if_no: UP950, LP800, or FP800 fuse fault.

sub_checks.if_yes: LCD panel or eDP flex fault.
SoC GPU output fault. Check PPVDD_AWAKE_GPU.

scenarios.meaning: Excessive current. Dead short or max power draw.

steps.action: Is board functional at this current?
steps.if_functional: May be under heavy load + charging. Monitor - should settle to 1.5-2.5A.

steps.if_found: Identify hot component, cross-reference with rails.json.

steps.measure: Diode mode: PPBUS_AON (C5704) -> PP3V8_AON -> PP5V_S2 -> PP3V3_S2
steps.process: Find which rail reads short. Thermal cam that area.
